metric_value: Not yet mature: no C compiler, 1MB program size limit, frequent OS patch tapes, OS/400 immature
notes: Users report ~30% less realized performance than IBM sizing tools predict

metric_name: HP HPPA Model 950 performance vs. HP 3000 Model 70
metric_value: 2.5x the performance of Model 70; CPU-upgradable to 955 and 960
notes: HP 955 ~3x performance of Model 70; Model 935 70% improvement but not expandable

metric_name: AS/400 B60 vs HP 3000 Model 70 DebitCredit performance
metric_value: AS/400 B60 offers ~25% less performance than HP 3000 Model 70; B70 approximates Model 70
notes: IBM RAMP-C rated B60 as <10% more than HPPA 950, but methodology disputed

metric_name: AS/400 conversion risk level for Stanley-Vidmar
metric_value: High risk: MIS staff retraining, software conversion, new sales analysis system from scratch, cost escalation foreseeable
notes: Aberdeen: AS/400 will absolutely involve more effort, risk, and total cost than HP upgrade

metric_value: Aberdeen recommends HP 950 as entry point; 955 if >25% growth per annum expected in 2 years
notes: HP 935 rejected due to non-expandability; box-swap vs. CPU-swap distinction key

metric_value: Partner Richard Stuckey stated HP TurboImage was most impressive system in IBM's tests; AA subsequently OEM'd HP systems
notes: Aberdeen used this as independent validation of HP superiority over AS/400

metric_value: Journaling and Checksum recommended for production systems can reduce throughput by up to 60%
notes: IBM RAMP-C benchmarks did not use journaling, making results illusory for real production

metric_value: MIPS invalid for AS/400 vs. HPPA comparison; AS/400 MIPS undisclosed by IBM; multiprocessor architecture makes definition unclear
notes: Aberdeen recommends DebitCredit/TPC as the only valid comparison metric

metric_name: Stanley-Vidmar HP expertise as intangible asset
metric_value: MIS staff expertise in HP MPE OS is a significant intangible asset; existing low-priority programs run in MPE V emulation indefinitely
notes: HP upgrade preserves institutional knowledge; AS/400 requires full MIS retraining

metric_name: Aberdeen vs. Gartner Group competitive differentiation
metric_value: Aberdeen: custom consulting from user interviews; Gartner: subscription research from supplier sources
notes: Aberdeen claims no single client large enough to influence research; neither IBM nor HP constitute substantial revenue

metric_name: HP Allbase SQL performance improvement projection
metric_value: Aberdeen expects Allbase SQL performance to improve 50% during 1990
notes: Viability prediction for relational performance improvement

metric_value: Aberdeen expects TurboImage performance on HPPA to improve 20% in 1990
notes: Sustained performance improvements over CISC models

metric_value: AS/400 lacks growth path today; multiple-AS/400 clustering not a reasonable alternative
notes: This proved partially wrong: AS/400 was extensively developed and as IBM i still active in 2026

metric_value: AS/400 evolved through iSeries, System i to IBM i on Power Systems; still actively supported in 2026 with 100,000+ installations
notes: Aberdeen underestimated IBM's commitment to evolving the AS/400 platform

metric_value: HP ended support December 31, 2010; Stromasys emulator released 2012 for legacy sites

metric_value: 935 70% capacity improvement over Model 70 but not expandable; 950 CPU-upgradable to 955/960 for higher initial cost

metric_value: Aberdeen normalized DebitCredit results across HP's and IBM's vendor-specific benchmark runs to enable cross-vendor comparison
